answer: On September 15, 2026, the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline joined settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Since normalized_tax_id can match multiple buyer and seller roles within the same closing, escrow disbursements were duplicated in finance.disbursement_recon_daily, overstating the escrow disbursement reconciliation variance for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, causing duplicate matches when the same tax ID appeared on multiple buyer and seller roles within a single closing
